roadmap.goal: On the System Settings -> API Keys tab, drop the always-redundant 'Created By' column from the listing and, when RBAC is enabled, replace it with a 'Scopes' column that surfaces per-key ApiKeyPermission rows. Backend slice: drop CreatedByUser from ApiKeyInfo / ApiKeyCreationResult DTOs, add a Scopes IReadOnlyList<ApiKeyScopeDto> to ApiKeyInfo, populate it inside ApiKeyService.GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject via a Dictionary<int, List<ApiKeyScopeDto>> built from apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll(). Frontend slice: drop createdByUser from IApiKeyInfo / IApiKeyCreationResult, add scopes: IApiKeyScope[] to IApiKeyInfo, replace the 'Created By' header+cell with a useRbac()-gated 'Scopes' header+cell that renders one chip per scope row (or 'Unrestricted' when scopes.length === 0), reusing the existing scopeDataLoaded lazy lookup for human-readable team/portfolio names. Documentation in docs/settings/apikeys.md is updated in the same slice as part of DoD. A type-level negative regression guard (.test-d.ts with a satisfies clause) prevents reintroduction of createdByUser on the DTO interfaces.
roadmap.overview: Two-step roadmap. The DISTILL governance note declares this feature is under one TDD slice, but the slice spans two stacks (C# backend DTO+service change with an integration test; TS frontend component+model+factory change with seven Vitest cases). Splitting along the stack boundary keeps each RED state tight and reviewable: step 01-01 RED-fails on backend HTTP contract assertions (M1.6) and a unit test on ApiKeyService; step 01-02 RED-fails on frontend DOM assertions (WS + M1.1..M1.5, M1.7, M1.8). The sister feature apikey-scope-ui-hidden-when-rbac-off shipped as a single step because it was a one-file UI conditional with no backend touch; this feature touches 4 production files across 2 stacks plus docs. Collapsing both into one step would couple two unrelated RED states (HTTP shape vs DOM) and force a long-lived RED window across the C# and TS suites simultaneously. The optional type-level regression guard from DISTILL section 'Test-factory regression guard' lives in step 01-02 (frontend type system).

validation.notes: Approved 2026-05-14 with zero blockers, zero high findings, zero low findings. All 9 DISTILL scenarios (WS + M1.1..M1.8) map to exactly one step. Walking skeleton in Phase 1 (step 01-02). Sizing of step 01-02 at boundary (8 scenarios) is justified: all DOM-assertions against the same component, single-cycle slice. Dependency direction correct (backend ships first). All AC observable through driving ports. Decomposition ratio 0.22 (well under 2.5 gate).

out_of_scope: Per-key scope editing from the list. Scopes remain write-once on creation; editing requires deletion + recreation. The new Scopes column is read-only. Candidate follow-up: apikey-scope-inline-edit.
Sorting and filtering on the Scopes column. The column is informational, not interactive.
Admin 'all keys' view. No such view exists today; introducing one would require a new RbacGuardRequirement.SystemAdmin endpoint and is unrelated to this feature.

Backend join to resolve team/portfolio display names. Per DISTILL Wave-decision reconciliation Q2, name resolution is performed on the frontend via the existing scopeDataLoaded lazy-load pattern. The Scopes cell falls back to 'Team #{id}' / 'Portfolio #{id}' when the lookup has not yet resolved.
Live RBAC-toggle detection. Page refresh required to pick up a runtime RBAC config flip; consistent with all other useRbac()-gated UI (Header, Settings, OverviewDashboard, Create API Key dialog).

Telemetry on deprecated-field reads. Per DISTILL Breaking-change classification, no deprecation counter is added; the field is removed in place on v1, no version bump, no public consumer contract documents createdByUser.

Screenshot regeneration for docs/assets/settings/apikeys.png. The doc text is the contract; the screenshot is illustration. Defer to a follow-up /update-docs run per DISTILL Documentation scaffold note.

phases.name: Replace Created By with conditional Scopes column
phases.description: One TDD slice split across two stack-local steps. Step 01-01: backend DTO surface (drop CreatedByUser from ApiKeyInfo / ApiKeyCreationResult, add Scopes array on ApiKeyInfo) plus service-layer population from apiKeyPermissionRepository, pinned by one HTTP integration test (M1.6) and one unit test on GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject. Step 01-02: frontend model alignment (drop createdByUser, add scopes), driving-adapter column swap with useRbac() gate and 'Unrestricted' default, factory refresh in MockApiServiceProvider, type-level regression guard, and the docs/settings/apikeys.md table edit. WS + M1.1..M1.5 + M1.7 + M1.8 are pinned by a new F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx; M1.6 is pinned by an addition to ApiKeyControllerHttpSmokeTests.cs.

steps.name: Backend: drop CreatedByUser DTO field, add Scopes array, populate from ApiKeyPermissionRepository
steps.description:
TDD execution for the backend slice.

**RED phase** — Add two failing tests against unmodified production code:

1. New test method in `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/API/Integration/ApiKeyControllerHttpSmokeTests.cs` exercising the existing `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` harness. Pattern: arrange one ApiKey row owned by the authenticated test user with one ApiKeyPermission row `(role=PortfolioAdmin, scopeType=Portfolio, scopeId=7)`. Act: HttpClient GET on `/api/v1/apikeys` (or `/api/latest/apikeys` consistent with the file's existing convention). Assert: response status 200; response body parses as a JSON array of length 1; the single element has a `scopes` property which is a JSON array of length 1 with `role=PortfolioAdmin`, `scopeType=Portfolio`, `scopeId=7`; the element does NOT have a `createdByUser` property (assert via JsonElement.TryGetProperty returning false). This encodes scenario M1.6.

2. New test method in `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/Services/Implementation/Auth/ApiKeyServiceTest.cs` exercising `ApiKeyService.GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject` through its public method only (no reflection, no internal state inspection). Pattern: arrange the existing Moq mock of `IApiKeyPermissionRepository` to return two permission rows for ApiKeyId=1 and one row for ApiKeyId=2 via `GetAll()`; arrange the `IApiKeyRepository` mock to return two ApiKey entities for the owner subject under test. Act: call `GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject(subject)`. Assert: the returned `IEnumerable<ApiKeyInfo>` has two elements; the element with Id=1 has Scopes.Count == 2 with the expected role/scopeType/scopeId triples; the element with Id=2 has Scopes.Count == 1. No assertion is made on private fields or internal collection types — only on the public ApiKeyInfo.Scopes contract.

Run `dotnet test` from `Lighthouse.Backend/` and confirm both new tests fail for the right reason (the response body still contains `createdByUser`; the `Scopes` property does not yet exist on ApiKeyInfo, so the test will not even compile until step 1 of the GREEN phase below — that compilation failure IS the RED state for this contract). Compile-failure-as-RED is acceptable here because the DTO change is the contract being driven by the test.

**GREEN phase** — Three production edits, smallest-diff-first:

1. `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Models/Auth/ApiKeyInfo.cs`: remove the `CreatedByUser` property entirely; add `public IReadOnlyList<ApiKeyScopeDto> Scopes { get; set; } = Array.Empty<ApiKeyScopeDto>();`. The default-empty assignment honors ADR-004 backwards-compatible default.

2. `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Models/Auth/ApiKeyCreationResult.cs`: remove the `CreatedByUser` property entirely. (The frontend consumer of this DTO is updated in step 01-02; the field's only reader is the React result-dialog component.)

3. `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Services/Implementation/Auth/ApiKeyService.cs::GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject`: materialise `apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll()` into a `Dictionary<int, List<ApiKeyScopeDto>>` keyed by `ApiKeyPermission.ApiKeyId`. For each ApiKey entity returned for the owner subject, populate the new `Scopes` property by looking up the dictionary (defaulting to an empty list when absent — per ADR-004). Do NOT introduce any new repository call, ordering guarantee, or sorting; the existing per-ApiKey filter by owner subject is unchanged. Remove the now-obsolete `CreatedByUser = ...` assignment on the ApiKeyInfo projection. In `CreateApiKeyAsync`, drop the `CreatedByUser = ...` assignment on the returned `ApiKeyCreationResult` (the property no longer exists). The `ApiKey.CreatedByUser` entity field stays untouched — `ApiKeyOwnerReconciliationSeeder` continues to read it.

Re-run `dotnet test` from `Lighthouse.Backend/`: both new tests now pass; all pre-existing tests pass (the most likely breakages are pre-existing `ApiKeyServiceTest` cases asserting on `CreatedByUser` or new ApiKeyInfo fixtures missing `Scopes` — neutralise them in this step by updating fixtures, not by deleting assertions).

**COMMIT phase** — Conventional commit `feat(apikey): replace CreatedByUser DTO field with per-key Scopes array`. Body references DISTILL feature-delta.md sections Inherited commitments and Breaking-change classification, notes the v1 breaking-in-place decision (no version bump), and reminds reviewers the entity field stays untouched.

**Quality gates (mandatory before COMMIT):**
- `dotnet build` from `Lighthouse.Backend/` — succeeds with zero warnings (TreatWarningsAsErrors is on; a leftover `CreatedByUser` reference will surface as a CS error, not a warning).
- `dotnet test` from `Lighthouse.Backend/` — every NUnit suite green; pre-existing `ApiKeyControllerHttpSmokeTests`, `ApiKeyServiceTest`, and `ApiKeyOwnerReconciliationSeederTest` all pass (the seeder test in particular MUST remain green — it exercises the entity field, not the DTO).
- SonarQube Cloud: no new issues introduced (the Dictionary materialisation must not push GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject over the cognitive-complexity threshold; if it does, extract a private helper `BuildScopeIndex(IEnumerable<ApiKeyPermission>)` per CLAUDE.md early-return / one-responsibility rules).

**Stryker.NET (per-feature mutation testing)** — Run Stryker on the `ApiKeyService` mutator scope after GREEN. Minimum kill rate per CLAUDE.md: 80%. Likely surviving mutants and how the new tests kill them: (a) replace the Dictionary lookup default with a non-empty list — killed by the M1.5 assertion in step 01-02 (zero rows -> 'Unrestricted'), surfaced at the backend layer by the unit test's two-key assertion shape; (b) inverted filter on ApiKeyId — killed by the per-key Scopes.Count differential between Id=1 and Id=2 in the new unit test.
steps.scenario_name: M1.6 (HTTP contract: response includes scopes array, drops createdByUser) + ApiKeyService unit test (GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject populates Scopes from apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll())
red_to_green_tests: GET /api/v1/apikeys returns response body whose elements have a scopes JSON array and no createdByUser property (M1.6)
GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject populates ApiKeyInfo.Scopes from apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll() filtered by ApiKeyId
GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject returns Scopes as an empty list when a key has zero ApiKeyPermission rows (ADR-004 default)
criteria: ApiKeyInfo has no CreatedByUser property and exposes IReadOnlyList<ApiKeyScopeDto> Scopes defaulting to an empty array.
ApiKeyCreationResult has no CreatedByUser property.

ApiKeyService.GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject materialises apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll() once into a Dictionary<int, List<ApiKeyScopeDto>> keyed by ApiKeyId and populates the Scopes property per ApiKeyInfo (no N+1 repository calls).
ApiKeyService.CreateApiKeyAsync no longer sets a CreatedByUser property on the returned ApiKeyCreationResult.
ApiKey entity field CreatedByUser is untouched; ApiKeyOwnerReconciliationSeederTest remains green without modification.

ApiKeyControllerHttpSmokeTests.cs gains one new test asserting the M1.6 contract (response status 200; body element has a scopes array of length 1 matching the seeded permission row; body element has no createdByUser property).
ApiKeyServiceTest.cs gains one new test asserting GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject populates Scopes from the permission repository and yields an empty Scopes list for keys with zero permission rows.
All new tests fail against unmodified production code (RED verified before any production edit; compile-failure-as-RED is acceptable because the DTO surface is the contract under test).
After production edits, all new and pre-existing backend tests pass.
dotnet build is clean: zero warnings (TreatWarningsAsErrors enforced).
dotnet test is green across the full backend suite.
Stryker.NET kill rate on the ApiKeyService mutation scope is >= 80%.
SonarQube Cloud introduces no new issues (bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, security hotspots) on this PR.
Conventional commit: `feat(apikey): replace CreatedByUser DTO field with per-key Scopes array`.
deliverables: Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Services/Implementation/Auth/ApiKeyService.cs (modified: +Dictionary build over apiKeyPermissionRepository.GetAll(), +Scopes population per ApiKeyInfo, -CreatedByUser assignment in two places)

Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/Services/Implementation/Auth/ApiKeyServiceTest.cs (modified: +1 NUnit test for GetApiKeysByOwnerSubject Scopes population; existing fixtures updated to drop CreatedByUser and default Scopes to empty)

steps.name: Frontend: drop Created By column, render conditional Scopes column with Unrestricted default, refresh test factory, add type-level regression guard, update docs
steps.description:
TDD execution for the frontend slice. Depends on step 01-01 because the production code change relies on the new `scopes` field arriving from the backend; however the tests in this step mock the API boundary, so 01-02 can be authored RED locally before 01-01 ships if developer convenience demands it. For commit ordering, 01-01 lands first so a partial revert always restores a self-consistent backend.

**RED phase** — Create one new test file and update three existing ones:

1. New file `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx`. Pattern: import `ApiKeysSettings` and the existing `createMockApiServiceContext` from `../../../tests/MockApiServiceProvider`; mirror the Wrapper / mock setup from the sibling `F_FE_1_CreateApiKeyDialogScope.test.tsx` (read it as a reference; do NOT modify it). Each test mocks `rbacService.getAuthorizationSummary`, `apiKeyService.getApiKeys`, `teamService.getTeams`, and `portfolioService.getPortfolios` per scenario:
   - WS + M1.4: RBAC on, one key 'CI Key' with one TeamAdmin/Team/42 scope row -> assert table renders, headers include 'Scopes', headers do NOT include 'Created By', the 'CI Key' row shows a scope entry corresponding to 'Team Admin' and team 42.
   - M1.1: RBAC off, one key 'CI Key' -> headers do NOT include 'Created By'.
   - M1.2: RBAC on, one key 'CI Key' with no scope rows -> headers do NOT include 'Created By'.
   - M1.3: RBAC off, one key -> headers do NOT include 'Scopes'.
   - M1.5: RBAC on, key 'Legacy Key' with zero scope rows -> the 'Legacy Key' row's Scopes cell shows a single entry labelled 'Unrestricted'.
   - M1.7: getAuthorizationSummary mocked as a pending (never-resolving) promise -> headers do NOT include 'Scopes' (PERMISSIVE_SUMMARY default; useRbac().isRbacEnabled === false while loading).
   - M1.8: RBAC on, teams lookup resolves to [{ id: 42, name: 'Platform' }], portfolios lookup rejects, key 'Mixed Key' with TeamAdmin/Team/42 and PortfolioAdmin/Portfolio/7 rows -> the team scope entry text contains 'Team Admin' and 'Platform' (no '#42'); the portfolio scope entry text contains 'Portfolio Admin' and 'Portfolio #7' (id-fallback).
   Use observable DOM assertions only (`screen.getByRole('columnheader', ...)`, `within(row).getByText(...)`, `queryByRole(...) === null`). NO assertions on component state, useRbac internals, or scopeDataLoaded boolean. NO `data-testid` introspection beyond the existing `api-keys-table` / row testIds.

2. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/ApiKeysSettings.test.tsx` — update existing fixtures: drop every `createdByUser` literal from `IApiKeyInfo` test objects, add `scopes: []` defaulting; remove any explicit 'Created By' assertions; add ONE new test asserting M1.1 column absence (RBAC off -> no 'Created By' header) for redundancy with F_FE_2_*. The five pre-existing cases (empty state, delete, dialog open, etc.) remain semantically intact.

3. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/CreateApiKeyDialogScope_RbacOff.test.tsx` — update fixtures: drop `createdByUser`, add `scopes: []` to every `IApiKeyInfo` test row. No behavioural assertions change.

4. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/F_FE_1_CreateApiKeyDialogScope.test.tsx` — same fixture refresh; do not change assertions.

5. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/tests/MockApiServiceProvider.ts` — in `createMockApiKeyService`, drop the `createdByUser` literal from any default `IApiKeyInfo` returned by the factory; default `scopes: []`. The factory output must satisfy `IApiKeyInfo` exactly with no `as`-casts.

6. New file `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/models/ApiKey/ApiKey.test-d.ts` (type-level regression guard per DISTILL section 'Test-factory regression guard', option 1 — preferred). Contains an unused `satisfies` expression: `({} as IApiKeyInfo) satisfies { id: number; name: string; description: string; createdAt: string; lastUsedAt: string | null; scopes: IApiKeyScope[] }` and a parallel one for `IApiKeyCreationResult`. The `satisfies` enumeration deliberately omits `createdByUser`; if a future PR re-adds the field to either interface, `tsc -b` (and thus `pnpm build`) fails. Zero runtime cost.

Run `pnpm test` (Vitest) — all new tests in F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx fail for the right reason against unmodified `ApiKeysSettings.tsx`: the 'Created By' header still exists, no 'Scopes' header is rendered, the Scopes cell does not exist, the Unrestricted label does not appear. The type-level test fails `tsc -b` because `IApiKeyInfo` still has `createdByUser`, which the `satisfies` clause does not enumerate. The fixture updates in (2)-(5) fail `tsc -b` against unmodified `ApiKey.ts` because `scopes` does not yet exist on the interface.

**GREEN phase** — Production edits in dependency order:

1. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/models/ApiKey/ApiKey.ts`: remove `createdByUser: string` from `IApiKeyInfo`; remove `createdByUser: string` from `IApiKeyCreationResult`; add `scopes: IApiKeyScope[]` to `IApiKeyInfo`. `IApiKeyScope` already exists in the same file (lines 27-31) and is reused verbatim.

2. `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/ApiKeysSettings.tsx` (table header + row body, lines 380-423):
   - Remove the `<TableCell>Created By</TableCell>` header (line ~386) and the corresponding `<TableCell>{key.createdByUser}</TableCell>` body cell (line ~397).
   - Import `useRbac` from `../../../hooks/useRbac` if not already imported in the surrounding component.
   - Inside the list-rendering component, call `const rbac = useRbac();` and gate a new `<TableCell>Scopes</TableCell>` header on `rbac.isRbacEnabled === true` (placed in the column position vacated by 'Created By', i.e. after Description). Same gate on the body cell.
   - Body cell renderer: when `key.scopes.length === 0`, render a single 'Unrestricted' label (Material UI Chip or Typography — match the visual idiom already used by neighbouring cells). When `key.scopes.length > 0`, render one chip per scope row using the existing `scopeDataLoaded` lazy lookup pattern (lines 95-128) to resolve team/portfolio names: render `${humanRole} \u00B7 ${resolvedName}` when the lookup completed and a name exists, otherwise `${humanRole} \u00B7 ${scopeTypeLabel} #${scopeId}`. `humanRole` is the existing role-label mapping already used in the Create dialog scope picker (e.g. 'TeamAdmin' -> 'Team Admin'); reuse it from its current location, do NOT duplicate.
   - Loading branch: `useRbac()` returns `isRbacEnabled: false` while pending (PERMISSIVE_SUMMARY default), which naturally hides the Scopes column per M1.7. No extra branch logic needed.
   - Do NOT introduce a second fetch of `/authorization/my-summary`. Do NOT block table rendering on the team / portfolio lookups. The Scopes header may render before the scope-name lookups resolve; cells fall back to `Team #{id}` / `Portfolio #{id}` until then.

3. `docs/settings/apikeys.md` (lines 50-58): remove the 'Created By' row from the column table; add a short paragraph (or a conditional row) describing the new RBAC-gated Scopes column with the 'Unrestricted' default per ADR-004, per the verbatim guidance in DISTILL section 'Documentation scaffold'.

Re-run `pnpm test` — all new and updated tests pass. Re-run `pnpm build` — `tsc -b` succeeds (the type-level test now passes because `IApiKeyInfo` matches the `satisfies` enumeration exactly), Biome reports zero errors and zero warnings, `vite build` completes.

**COMMIT phase** — Conventional commit `feat(apikey): replace Created By column with conditional Scopes column on API Keys listing`. Body references DISTILL feature-delta.md sections User stories US-1..US-3 and Documentation scaffold; calls out the type-level regression guard.

**Quality gates (mandatory before COMMIT):**
- `pnpm test` from `Lighthouse.Frontend/` — full Vitest suite green including F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx (>= 7 cases pinning WS + M1.1 + M1.2 + M1.3 + M1.4 + M1.5 + M1.7 + M1.8).
- `pnpm build` from `Lighthouse.Frontend/` — zero TS errors (`tsc -b`), zero Biome errors / warnings (prebuild hook), `vite build` completes.
- F_FE_1_CreateApiKeyDialogScope.test.tsx remains GREEN; CreateApiKeyDialogScope_RbacOff.test.tsx remains GREEN.
- ApiKey.test-d.ts compiles successfully; deliberately revert IApiKeyInfo to include createdByUser locally as a sanity check — `tsc -b` must fail — then restore.
- SonarQube Cloud: no new issues (in particular, no new cognitive-complexity hotspot on the scope-cell renderer; if it surfaces, extract a `renderScopeCell(scope, teamLookup, portfolioLookup): ReactNode` helper at the same file scope — pure function, no hooks).
- docs/settings/apikeys.md updated within the same commit (DISTILL DoD requirement).

**Stryker mutation testing (TypeScript/React, per-feature)** — Run Stryker on the `ApiKeysSettings.tsx` mutator scope after GREEN. Minimum kill rate per CLAUDE.md: 80%. Likely surviving mutants and how the new tests kill them: (a) flip the `rbac.isRbacEnabled` conditional to always-true — killed by M1.3 / M1.7 (Scopes header must be absent when RBAC is off or loading); (b) flip the `scopes.length === 0` check to `=== 1` — killed by M1.4 (multi-scope row renders two entries, not Unrestricted) + M1.5 (zero rows renders Unrestricted); (c) drop the team-name resolution branch — killed by M1.8 (resolved 'Platform' name asserted in the team row); (d) drop the portfolio-fallback branch — killed by M1.8 (id-fallback 'Portfolio #7' asserted in the portfolio row).
steps.test_file: Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx (plus fixture refresh in ApiKeysSettings.test.tsx, CreateApiKeyDialogScope_RbacOff.test.tsx, F_FE_1_CreateApiKeyDialogScope.test.tsx) + Lighthouse.Frontend/src/models/ApiKey/ApiKey.test-d.ts (new, type-level regression guard)
steps.scenario_name: WS + M1.1 + M1.2 + M1.3 + M1.4 + M1.5 + M1.7 + M1.8 (walking-skeleton.feature + milestone-1-column-replacement.feature excluding M1.6 which is owned by step 01-01)
red_to_green_tests: renders Scopes column header when RBAC is enabled and 'CI Key' shows a Team Admin / Team #42 entry (WS + M1.4)
does NOT render 'Created By' column header when RBAC is disabled (M1.1)
does NOT render 'Created By' column header when RBAC is enabled (M1.2 regression pin both ways)
does NOT render 'Scopes' column header when RBAC is disabled (M1.3)
renders 'Unrestricted' label in the Scopes cell when the key has zero scope rows (M1.5)
does NOT render 'Scopes' column header while authorization summary fetch is pending (M1.7)
renders resolved team name 'Platform' for scopeId=42 and falls back to 'Portfolio #7' when portfolio lookup is unavailable (M1.8)
MockApiServiceProvider.createMockApiKeyService factory returns IApiKeyInfo objects without createdByUser and with scopes: [] by default
ApiKey.test-d.ts type-level satisfies clause fails compilation if createdByUser is reintroduced on IApiKeyInfo or IApiKeyCreationResult
criteria: IApiKeyInfo has no createdByUser property and exposes scopes: IApiKeyScope[].
IApiKeyCreationResult has no createdByUser property.
ApiKeysSettings.tsx no longer renders a 'Created By' column header or body cell on any branch.

ApiKeysSettings.tsx renders a 'Scopes' column header and body cells only when useRbac().isRbacEnabled === true; loading and fetch-failure default to hidden via the existing PERMISSIVE_SUMMARY behaviour.
When a key has zero scope rows, the Scopes cell renders an 'Unrestricted' label (ADR-004 default).

When a key has one or more scope rows, the Scopes cell renders one entry per row using the existing scopeDataLoaded lazy lookup for human-readable team / portfolio names, with id-fallback ('Team #{id}' / 'Portfolio #{id}') when the lookup is unresolved.
No second fetch of /authorization/my-summary is introduced; useRbac() is the single RBAC source of truth in this component.
F_FE_2_ApiKeyListScopesColumn.test.tsx contains >= 7 distinct Vitest tests covering WS + M1.1 + M1.2 + M1.3 + M1.4 + M1.5 + M1.7 + M1.8.
Every new test fails on unmodified production code (RED state confirmed before any production edit).

Pre-existing ApiKeysSettings.test.tsx, F_FE_1_CreateApiKeyDialogScope.test.tsx, and CreateApiKeyDialogScope_RbacOff.test.tsx remain green after fixture refresh; the only edits are fixture-shape updates (drop createdByUser, add scopes: []) and one new M1.1 column-absence test in ApiKeysSettings.test.tsx.
MockApiServiceProvider.createMockApiKeyService factory output satisfies IApiKeyInfo without `as`-casts and without createdByUser.
ApiKey.test-d.ts compiles successfully against the updated interfaces and would fail compilation if either interface re-acquired createdByUser (verified by a local mutation-and-revert sanity check).

docs/settings/apikeys.md is updated in the same commit: the 'Created By' row is removed; the new conditional Scopes column is documented with the 'Unrestricted' default per ADR-004 (DISTILL Documentation scaffold mandate).
pnpm test (full Vitest suite) is green.
pnpm build is clean: zero TypeScript errors, zero Biome errors, zero warnings.
Stryker.NET (TypeScript) kill rate on the ApiKeysSettings.tsx mutation scope >= 80%.
SonarQube Cloud introduces no new issues on this PR.
Conventional commit: `feat(apikey): replace Created By column with conditional Scopes column on API Keys listing`.
deliverables: Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/ApiKeysSettings.tsx (modified: -Created By header/cell, +useRbac-gated Scopes header/cell with Unrestricted default and scopeDataLoaded name resolution)
Lighthouse.Frontend/src/pages/Settings/ApiKeys/ApiKeysSettings.test.tsx (modified: fixture refresh + 1 new M1.1 column-absence test)
Lighthouse.Frontend/src/tests/MockApiServiceProvider.ts (modified: createMockApiKeyService factory drops createdByUser, defaults scopes: [])
